---
title: "2.7 while Loops"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### `while` repeats while a condition is true

A <span class="term">while loop</span> (while 循环) repeatedly executes a block as long as its Boolean condition remains true.

![Loop overview](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-7-while-loops/loops.png){fig-align="center" width="36%"}

```java
while (condition)
{
    // repeated statements
}
```

When the condition becomes false, execution continues after the loop.

## Flow of a While Loop

### Test before each repetition

![While loop flow](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-7-while-loops/WhileLoopFlow.png){fig-align="center" width="38%"}

Important rule: if the condition is false before the first check, the loop body runs zero times.

## Three Steps

### Initialize, test, update

![Three steps to writing a loop](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-7-while-loops/loop3steps.png){fig-align="center" width="42%"}

```java
int count = 1;              // initialize
while (count <= 5)          // test
{
    System.out.println(count);
    count++;                // update
}
```

Missing the update often causes an infinite loop.

## Code Task

### Print odd numbers with `while`

Starter:

```java
public class PrintOdds
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        int num = 1;
        while (num <= 9)
        {
            System.out.println(num);
            num += 2;
        }
    }
}
```

Modify it to print odd numbers from `1` through `15`.

## Quick Check

### Trace a `while` loop

What is printed?

```java
int x = 1;
while (x < 5)
{
    System.out.print(x + " ");
    x = x + 2;
}
```

Answer:

```text
1 3
```

The next value would be `5`, and `5 < 5` is false.

## Trace Table

### Track the variable after each update

![Trace table](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-7-while-loops/traceTable.png){fig-align="center" width="30%"}

Trace columns should show:

::: {.tight-list}
- loop variable before the condition
- whether the condition is true
- output or body effect
- loop variable after update
:::

## Common Errors

### Infinite loops and off-by-one errors

```java
int i = 1;
while (i <= 5)
{
    System.out.println(i);
}
```

Problem: `i` never changes.

Fix:

```java
i++;
```

Put the update where every successful loop pass can reach it.

## Input-Controlled Loops

### Sentinel values decide when to stop

```java
Scanner scan = new Scanner(System.in);
int number = scan.nextInt();

while (number != -1)
{
    System.out.println("You entered " + number);
    number = scan.nextInt();
}
```

`-1` is the <span class="term">sentinel</span> (哨兵值): it signals that input is finished.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Turtle squares with repeated code

Use a `while` loop to replace repeated movement commands.

```java
int sides = 0;
while (sides < 4)
{
    yertle.forward();
    yertle.turn(90);
    sides++;
}
```

Class goal: identify the repeated square-drawing pattern, then control it with initialize-test-update.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that a `while` loop tests before each repetition
- write the initialize-test-update structure
- trace loop variable changes with a table
- identify infinite-loop and off-by-one mistakes
- explain how a sentinel controls an input loop
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
